title: Bitfarms Announces U.S. Redomiciliation and Rebrand to Keel Infrastructure
text: Bitfarms announced board-approved plan to relocate from Canada to Delaware with shareholder approval scheduled for March 20, 2026. Transition aims to complete by April 1, 2026. Company will rebrand as Keel Infrastructure trading under ticker symbol KEEL on both Nasdaq and Toronto Stock Exchange. CEO Ben Gagnon explained symbolism stating keel is structural backbone of vessel—largely unseen but critical foundation. Move emphasizes company pivot toward HPC/AI infrastructure development rather than Bitcoin mining. According to management, benefits include expanded access to capital, improved index eligibility and simplified messaging for U.S. investors. Each Bitfarms share converts to one Keel Infrastructure share. Company's New York office becomes principal executive office though Canadian and U.S. operations continue unchanged. Bitfarms repaid $300 million Macquarie debt facility on February 5, 2026 leveraging strong liquidity of $698 million net as of February 5, granting financial flexibility for pursuing cost-effective project financing.
